name: loop-me
description: Recurring workflow spec designer — stateful grilling session whose only output is the spec, not the implementation. Hand off to OMO /goal or incremental-implementation.

positive_triggers.reason: Explicit slash command trigger (disable-model-invocation means user MUST invoke manually)

positive_triggers.input: I keep manually triaging GitHub notifications every morning — I want to specify a workflow for it so I don't have to do it by hand anymore
positive_triggers.reason: Recurring activity + manual labor + user wants it spec'd (not implemented yet) → loop-me domain

positive_triggers.input: Every Monday I review last week's merged PRs and draft this week's priorities — let's write a spec for this weekly loop
positive_triggers.reason: Explicit recurring cadence (every Monday) + user wants a workflow spec for the loop

negative_triggers.reason: One-shot debug, no recurring activity → debugging-and-error-recovery

negative_triggers.input: Help me design a REST API for managing tasks
negative_triggers.reason: Single design exploration, not a recurring loop spec → brainstorming or spec-driven-development

negative_triggers.input: Implement the new checkout flow with cart, payment, confirmation
negative_triggers.reason: Multi-step implementation, not a spec-design session → incremental-implementation

behavioral_evals.scenario: User says '/loop-me' without arguments and has an empty NOTES.md
expected_behavior: Agent reads NOTES.md, finds it empty/thin
Agent does NOT start writing workflow specs immediately
Agent enters grilling mode to interview the user about their 'world': tools they use, channels they process, their own terminology
Agent asks ONE question at a time, each with a recommended answer
Agent sharpens fuzzy terms into canonical ones and writes them back to NOTES.md
Only after NOTES is populated does agent suggest candidate loops from user's world

behavioral_evals.scenario: User describes a recurring activity they want spec'd (e.g. 'I do X every morning')
expected_behavior: Agent enters grilling mode: ONE question per turn, each with a recommended answer
Agent uses Vocabulary only when grilling surfaces need: Trigger (event vs schedule), Checkpoint (HIL points), Push right (defer until last), Brief (decision-ready summary, not raw output)
Agent does NOT impose structure — workflows may have no AI, no Checkpoint, no Schedule unless grilling shows they need one
Agent writes spec to workflows/<name>.md at workspace root (NOT .planning/<id>/workflows/)
Spec includes all 7 required sections: Trigger / Inputs / Steps / Checkpoints / Outputs / Failure modes / Definition of done
Definition of done: 'an implementer agent could build it without asking a single question' — agent self-verifies this before declaring session done
Agent suggests hand-off: OMO /goal (continuous execution) or incremental-implementation (build the script) or writing-skills (capture as meta-skill)

notes: disable-model-invocation: true on the SKILL.md frontmatter — agent must NOT auto-invoke this skill, only user /loop-me triggers it. Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
